title: Jussiaea haenkeana
taxonomy: Jussiaea haenkeana is classified as a species within the genus Jussiaea and the family Onagraceae. It belongs to the family of evening primroses, which is a family of flowering plants. The genus Jussiaea is a monotypic genus, meaning it only contains one species, Jussiaea haenkeana. This species is native to South America and is known for its unique characteristics and growth habits. Jussiaea haenkeana is a member of the order Myrtales, a large and diverse order of flowering plants.
uses: Jussiaea haenkeana, also known as the water chamomile, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. It is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties.
